message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="orange" summary="Mini-retro: contrast threshold adjustment, Tidy First candidates">
## Mini-Retro: Step 6 — Lea's observations

**What went well:**
- The project component CSS is comprehensive and follows established patterns closely -- list items, edit forms, detail page, action items all mirror the conventions from inbox/next-actions/contexts.
- The stalled project indicator design is one of the best UI patterns we've shipped: pill badge on the list + notice box on detail, text-based (not color-only), visually distinct without being alarming. Aligns perfectly with the "calm over urgent" UX principle.
- The breadcrumb on project detail is simple and effective for the Trunk Test.
- Heading hierarchy on the detail page (h1 project title > h2 "Next Actions" / "Completed Actions") with `aria-labelledby` sections is exemplary.
- Focus management JS included from day one -- retro lesson applied.

**What could improve:**
- The stalled flag contrast issue (`--color-warning` on `--color-warning-light`) was flagged as Minor by me but escalated to Blocking by Heydon. This validates that contrast issues on small text should probably be Blocking by default in my reviews. I'll adjust my threshold going forward: any text contrast violation on `--font-size-xs` or `--font-size-sm` gets BLOCKING.
- Redundant select styles continue to accumulate (now on 3 pages: next-actions, inbox, project-detail). This is a clear candidate for a Tidy First pass -- extract the common flex-select pattern.
- Missing forced-colors rules on ghost buttons continue to accumulate. Same Tidy First candidate.

**Process observation:**
- The `--color-success` token is annotated in our tokens layer as "icons/indicators only -- fails AA for normal text" but it was still used for text in `.project-item__complete-button:hover`. This suggests the annotation in the CSS comment isn't enough -- the Driver may not re-read the tokens layer before using a token. Consider adding this as a checklist item: "Check token contrast annotations before using semantic colors for text."

For Step 7 (Clarify as Project): This extends the clarify form on inbox items to support creating a project + first next action. The CSS work should be modest -- likely extending the existing `.inbox-item__clarify` pattern with an additional form state. I'll send guidance once we kick off.

</teammate-message>

content.text:
Lea's mini-retro is for Step 6. She mentions Step 7 at the end but hasn't provided a verdict yet. Her process observations about contrast thresholds and token annotations are valuable.

I've already sent her a detailed redirect to Step 7 with the specific CSS changes to review. Waiting for her verdict along with the other 5 pending reviewers.

Current Step 7 consensus: 2 of 9 approved (Luca, Kent). Carson is the Driver (implicit). 6 reviewers pending.
